front: Work: this haibun travelogue opens 'The months and days are the travelers of eternity' and recounts a journey with companion Kawai Sora
back: The Narrow Road to the Deep North (Matsuo Basho)
work: The Narrow Road to the Deep North

front: Work: in this travelogue, 'summer grass' is all that remains of warriors' dreams
back: The Narrow Road to the Deep North (Matsuo Basho)
work: The Narrow Road to the Deep North

front: Work: at the end of this travelogue, the author feels 'like a clam ripped from its shell' while boarding a boat as 'autumn was deepening'
back: The Narrow Road to the Deep North (Matsuo Basho)
work: The Narrow Road to the Deep North

front: Work: 'The old pond / a frog jumps in / sound of water'
Work: this critical anthology compares/juxtaposes pairs of similar poems by different authors; named for a children's matching game
Work: this travel diary describes giving food to 'a child crying in the autumn wind'; its title object is found 'in windy fields of memory / piercing like a knife'
back: Records of a Weather-Exposed Skeleton (Matsuo Basho)

front: Author: this haiku master's death poem reads 'Sick on my journey / only my dreams will wander / these desolate moors'
Author: this poet took his pen name from a banana tree planted by disciples outside his hut; previous pen names were Sobo and Tosei ('Green Peach')
Author: this poet advocated for karumi (lightness) and the principle 'awakening to the high, returning to the low'; influenced by Zen Buddhism and Daoism
Author: this poet coined the term haibun for the form combining prose and haiku
Author: this poet punned on a word meaning both 'wait' and 'pine' in a poem repeatedly exclaiming 'Pine Islands!'
Author: a poem by this Japanese poet describing the Milky Way 'arched' over a 'stormy sea' inspired the conclusion of a 1937 novel
